question_html: Write a paragraph on ‘Youth Leadership’ in 10 sentences. 10
solution_html:
Youth Leadership

Youth leadership refers to the capacity of young individuals to lead and inspire positive change in their communities and beyond. Youth leadership brings fresh perspectives, energy, and innovation to the forefront. Young leaders are often driven by a desire for change, challenging the status quo and pushing for solutions that address contemporary issues like climate change, social justice, and technological advancement. Their adaptability and willingness to embrace new ideas enable them to navigate complex challenges with creativity and resilience. Moreover, young leaders tend to be more inclusive, valuing diverse voices and fostering collaboration across different sectors and communities. While they may lack experience, their passion and commitment can inspire others bridging generational gaps and mobilizing collective action. By empowering young leaders, society benefits from their forward-thinking approach and their ability to connect with peers, shaping a more dynamic and inclusive future. Furthermore, their digital fluency allows them to leverage technology in ways that make leadership more accessible and efficient. Youth leadership has the potential to redefine traditional practices, prioritizing innovation and sustainability. Ultimately, young leaders hold the promise of driving lasting change in a world that increasingly values adaptability and forward-thinking solutions.

Marks Distribution:

Highest Mark --------------------09 Marks

(A) For Title (Youth Leadership) -------- 01 Mark

(B) Topic Sentence (Introduction) ---------------------------01 Mark

Youth leadership refers to the capacity of young individuals to lead and inspire positive change in their communities and beyond.

C) Context Explanation (Youth Leadership) ( 06 points)------06 Marks
‎• It infuses fresh perspectives, energy, and innovation into the leadership landscape.
‎• Young leaders are motivated by a desire for change and often challenge the status quo on issues like climate change and social justice.
‎• Their adaptability and openness to new ideas enable them to tackle complex challenges creatively and resiliently.
‎• They are more inclusive, promoting collaboration and valuing diverse voices across sectors.
‎• Despite their lack of experience, their passion can mobilize collective action and bridge generational gaps.
‎• Empowering young leaders benefits society through their forward-thinking approaches and ability to connect with peers.
‎• Their digital fluency enhances leadership accessibility and efficiency.
‎• Youth leadership has the potential to transform traditional practices by prioritizing innovation and sustainability.

D) Concluding Sentence ------------01 Mark
‎Ultimately, young leaders hold the promise of driving lasting change in a world that increasingly values adaptability and forward-thinking solutions.

Instructions:

1. For every 3 spelling/grammatical mistakes, 01 mark has to be deducted.

2. We will consider up to 12 sentences. However, if a student writes more than 12 sentences but less than double (20 sentences), 50% of the deserving marks must be deducted. If it becomes double (20 sentences), full marks have to be deducted. Comment: Write within 10 sentences.

3. Students must write within a para. If a student writes more than one para, half of the deserving marks must be deducted. (Comment: Write within single/one para).

4. For writing only the title of the paragraph bears no mark. (comment: Write the Title/ Youth Leadership)

question_html:
Fill in the gaps with suitable words: 5

One of the (a)________ of water in our country is the rivers. Rivers are (b)_________ in our life, literature, economy and culture. But are the rivers in (c)_______ shape? Unfortunately, they are not. A few are already dead and several are going through the (d)_________ of death. The river Buriganga is an example of a (e)________ river.
solution_html:
(a) sources (b) everywhere (c) good (d) pangs (e) dying

Instructions:

Any mistake bears no mark. However, mistakes regarding capital and small letters can be overlooked.

question_html:
Fill in the gaps with the clues given in the box. There are more words than necessary. Make any grammatical changes (if necessary). 5

Junk food is very (a) ______ all over the world. People don’t have time to prepare or (b) ______ or cook food, they are in a hurry. Moreover, outside food seems to be very (c) ______. Hunger is the best (d) ______. So people (e) _____ readymade food to raw food. (f)______ or food value matters little. This’s why traditional foods are on the (g) ______. Food habit varies from country to country even though some items are very (h)______ in demand. And food habits are formed on the basis of (i)______ in a particular region. That’s why (j) ______ has a role in it.
solution_html:
(a) popular (f) Calorie

(b) process (g) wane

(c) tasty (h) high

(d) sauce (i) availability

(e) prefer (j) geography

Instructions:

Any mistake bears no mark. However, mistakes regarding capital and small letters can be overlooked.

question_html:
Write meaningful sentences of your own with each of the following words: 10

Leisurely, Oblivious, Contemplation, Empowerment, Kleptocratic, Harmony, Impertinent, Trustworthy, Abridge, Degradation
solution_html:
Leisurely (Adjective): In South Africa, greetings are leisurely and are marked by good cheers.

Oblivious (Adjective): He remained completely oblivious to the noise around him while reading his book.

Contemplation (Noun): After long contemplation, she finally decided to pursue her lifelong dream.

Empowerment (Noun): Education provides empowerment, giving individuals the ability to improve their lives.

Kleptocratic (Adjective): Citizens suffered under the kleptocratic regime that stole national resources for personal gain.

Harmony (Noun): Different communities lived in harmony and valued each other's traditions and beliefs.

Impertinent (Adjective): His impertinent question surprised everyone and created an awkward silence in the room.

Trustworthy (Adjective): She proved herself trustworthy and completed every task with honesty and dedication.

Abridge (Verb): The editor had to abridge the long report to meet the required length.

Degradation (Noun): Pollution caused environmental degradation and damaged ecosystems worldwide.

Instructions:

(i) Any correct and logically meaningful sentence will be accepted. (Sentences with weak meaning will not be allowed.)

(ii) For any grammatical or structural mistake, full mark for this item must be deducted. Examiners must underline and correct the mistake(s) in the scripts.

(iii) For 02 spelling mistakes, 01 mark will be deducted, for 01 spelling mistake, 0.5 marks will be deducted from that very sentence.
‎(iv) For changing the part of speech of the given word by adding a suffix or prefix, the full marks have to be deducted from the very number. But students can use any form if the given word is a verb (depending on the subject-verb agreement, tense, person, number, etc.).

question_html:
Translate the following paragraph into English: 5

শিক্ষা আমাদের জীবনে অর্থপূর্ণভাবে কাজ করার জন্য জ্ঞান ও একগুচ্ছ দক্ষতা দেয়। আমরা যখন পড়তে ও লিখতে শিখি, তখন আত্মবিশ্বাসের একটি মাত্রা অর্জন করি। আমরা নিজের মতো করে চিন্তা করতে শিখি এবং আমাদের ভাবনা প্রকাশ করি; আমরা অন্যদের সঙ্গে যোগাযোগ করার ও আমাদের কাজকর্ম সুষ্ঠুভাবে পরিচালনার দক্ষতা অর্জন করি। শিক্ষা আমাদের স্বাধীনভাবে চিন্তা করতে এবং নিজস্ব মতামত গঠন করতে সহায়তা করে । আমরা পৃথিবী সম্পর্কে যত বেশি জানতে পারি, এর ভালো দিকগুলি তত বেশি উপলব্ধি করি ।
solution_html:
অনুবাদ: Education gives us knowledge and a set of abilities to function meaningfully in life. As we learn how to read and write, we gain a degree of self-confidence. We learn to think for ourselves and articulate our thoughts. We pick up skills to communicate with others and manage our affairs well. Education helps us think independently and make our own opinions. As we know more about the world, we appreciate the good things it offers us.

Instructions:
‎* For accurate translation using grammar, punctuation, and sentence structure, students will get full marks. [05 marks for translating 05 sentences correctly]

* For every 03 grammatical/spelling errors 01 mark has to be deducted.

question_html:
Add at least five more sentences to complete the following story. 5

Ritu was rushing to the office when she noticed a bright yellow umbrella resting against a bench at the bus stop. It looked almost new, its handle still shiny, yet no one around seemed to claim it. Curiosity made her pick it up, and she wondered who might have forgotten something so cheerful on a cloudy morning. A small name tag inside revealed only the word Ayaan, written in neat handwriting...............
solution_html:
The Forgotten Umbrella

Ritu was rushing to the office when she noticed a bright yellow umbrella resting against a bench at the bus stop. It looked almost new, its handle still shiny, yet no one around seemed to claim it. Curiosity made her pick it up, and she wondered who might have forgotten something so cheerful on a cloudy morning. A small name tag inside revealed only the word Ayaan, written in neat handwriting. Feeling oddly responsible, she decided to keep it safe until she could return it. That afternoon, she placed a note on the bus stop pole, hoping the owner might see it. The next day, a young man approached her with a relieved smile, holding her note in his hand. He thanked her sincerely, explaining that the umbrella had been a gift from his late sister. Ritu returned it gently, realizing the item meant far more than she had imagined. As she walked away, she felt grateful for being part of a brief moment of someone else’s healing.

Marks Distribution

Highest mark---------------05 Marks

(i) Title (Any logical one) ----(01 Mark has to be deducted for not writing the title) (The Forgotten Umbrella)

(ii) Story (minimum new 5 sentences) 1×5=05 Marks

{If a student adds or writes at least 5-7 new sentences using the given part and completes the story with a proper description without any grammatical or spelling mistakes, he/she will get full marks}.

Instructions:

(i) If a student doesn’t write the given part, 01 mark has to be deducted (comment: write the given part first).

(ii) Students can write the story in any logical way (sample answer isn’t fixed).

(iii) The given part tells us that the story is the incidents of the Past. So, it is normal that the student will develop the story using Past Tense but it can be described in Present Tense and future Tense also by using direct speech.

(iv) If a student doesn’t write the Title, 01 mark has to be deducted (comment: write the Title/ The Forgotten Umbrella)
